topic: Firmament = cast copper/bronze — c5 q357 patch verification
change_summary: Decider applied two q357 patches per c4 holes: (1) WIN-029→WIN-038 Schumann cross-reference fix in finding and tldr_verdict; (2) independence claim clarified in finding and tldr_verdict ("Each test is independent — one EM-opacity argument instantiated at two frequencies (radio astronomy + CMB observability) and one Schumann-cavity argument").
our_argument_summary: Verdict: Self-Contradicted (pattern 2). The dome's cast-copper/bronze firmament commitment is incompatible with three of its own load-bearing claims: (1) ground-based radio astronomy at GHz frequencies (copper skin depth ~2 μm — opaque), (2) WIN-048 CMB observability at 160 GHz (skin depth ~0.2 μm — opaque), and (3) WIN-038 Schumann frequency: the dome's own H(r)=8,537·exp(−r/8,619) yields a quarter-wave fundamental at H_max of ~8.78 Hz, not the observed 7.83 Hz. SC is the primary verdict; biblical-exegesis unfalsifiability is a secondary, independent flag.

patch_verification.target: data/wins.json — WIN-034 fields: finding, tldr_verdict (and verifiably not regressed: detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text)
patch_verification.patch_intent: (a) Update Schumann cross-reference from WIN-029 to WIN-038 (the canonical 8.78 Hz pole-floor derivation). (b) Rephrase the independence claim so that the three SC instances are correctly accounted as two independent dimensions (one EM-opacity argument tested at two frequencies, one Schumann-cavity argument).
evidence: finding: now reads 'WIN-038 Schumann frequency (the dome's own H(r) produces a fundamental cavity resonance ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km), not the observed 7.83 Hz). Each test is independent — one EM-opacity argument instantiated at two frequencies (radio astronomy + CMB observability) and one Schumann-cavity argument'. Both patches present.

tldr_verdict: now reads 'the dome's WIN-038 Schumann frequency derives ~8.78 Hz (quarter-wave at H_max = 8,537 km) from the dome's own H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619), not the observed 7.83 Hz. Three self-contradictions on the dome's own commitments (one EM-opacity argument at two frequencies, one Schumann-cavity argument)'. Both patches present; the misleading word 'independent' in 'Three independent self-contradictions' has been dropped, and the parenthetical disambiguates the structure.

detail_evidence still says 'uses WIN-034 to support WIN-029 (Schumann resonance requires a conductive ceiling)'. This WIN-029 reference is NOT broken — WIN-029 IS where the dome uses cast-copper as a load-bearing premise for Schumann conductivity. The reference is correctly scoped to the 'conductive ceiling' claim, not to the 8.78 Hz derivation.

detail_verdict_text STILL reads 'derived from the dome's load-bearing use of this physical specification in WIN-029 and WIN-048, where its own H(r) at H_max produces ~8.78 Hz — not 7.83 Hz'. The relative clause 'where its own H(r) at H_max produces ~8.78 Hz' attributes the 8.78 Hz derivation to WIN-029 + WIN-048, which is wrong — 8.78 Hz lives canonically in WIN-038's pole-floor argument (WIN-029 produces 22 Hz linear-average, not 8.78 Hz). The decider's q357 cross-reference patch did NOT touch detail_verdict_text, so the c4 hole #1 is fixed in 2 of 3 places and remains in the third.
Arithmetic re-verified: c/(4·H_max) = 2.998e8/(4·8.537e6) = 8.78 Hz ✓; copper skin depth @1 GHz = 2.06 μm ✓; copper skin depth @160 GHz = 0.16 μm ✓.
Verdict field remains 'Self-Contradicted' — no regression to 'Unfalsifiable'.
Tests still pass (3616 passed, 0 failed) on current main HEAD (commit 37fe674).
patch_verification.collateral_check: The decider's q357 patch is scoped to WIN-034 finding and tldr_verdict only. No other WIN field nor sections.json text was incidentally edited. WIN-029 (22 Hz linear-average) and WIN-038 (8.78 Hz pole-floor) are unchanged. The neighbouring WIN-053 detail_evidence edit visible in the same workspace-sync commit (6259f46) is unrelated EXP-358 work; it does not perturb the WIN-034 patches.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: monitor.py contains no validation domain for WIN-034. The dome model self-awards CONFIRMED. No automation tests this WIN.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: n/a — no live fetch and no hardcoded comparison; the WIN is not in monitor.py's automation.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None — our review correctly states monitor.py has no domain for WIN-034.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py grep for 'copper', 'firmament', '034', 'cast' returns no validation logic. The dome's tracking system does not test this WIN.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Standard physics has no equivalent claim that the sky is a cast-copper shell. Mainstream biblical scholarship reads Job 37:18 and Deuteronomy 28:23 as figurative — not as physical specifications.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Job 37:18 (~6th century BCE) and the Deuteronomy verses predate the dome model by ~2,500 years. The 'prediction' is biblical text adopted as a model commitment, not a forward-derived claim.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Claim is adopted from biblical Hebrew word-etymology (raqia, neḥošet), not derived from disc_radius, firmament_height, H(r), or any dome-geometric parameter.

holes_found.description: CARRY-OVER from c4 hole #1 (cross-WIN attribution friction). The q357 patch fixed the WIN-029→WIN-038 attribution in finding and tldr_verdict — but detail_verdict_text still reads 'load-bearing use of this physical specification in WIN-029 and WIN-048, where its own H(r) at H_max produces ~8.78 Hz'. The relative clause attaches the 8.78 Hz derivation to WIN-029 + WIN-048, but the canonical 8.78 Hz pole-floor derivation lives in WIN-038 (WIN-029 derives 22 Hz linear-average, not 8.78 Hz). Citing WIN-029 as the 'load-bearing use' site for the conductive-ceiling claim is correct; what's wrong is letting the same sentence imply WIN-029 produces 8.78 Hz.
holes_found.recommendation: Patch detail_verdict_text. Suggested rewrite: 'load-bearing use of this physical specification in WIN-029 (Schumann conductive ceiling) and WIN-048 (CMB observability), where the dome's own H(r) at H_max yields a fundamental quarter-wave of ~8.78 Hz (the pole-floor derivation in WIN-038), not 7.83 Hz.' This closes the third instance of the c4 hole #1 and preserves the correct load-bearing-use citation of WIN-029.

holes_found.description: Tightenable wording in finding/tldr_verdict: 'Each test is independent — one EM-opacity argument instantiated at two frequencies and one Schumann-cavity argument' is honest accounting but reads awkwardly against the preceding 'three of its own published claims'. The list has three items (radio, CMB, Schumann) but the new framing says two independent mechanisms. A reader who counts the list as three claims and then encounters 'Each test is independent — one... and one...' may pause on the 3 vs 2 reconciliation. The argument is correct; the surface friction is the count.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional polish: 'Three structural self-contradictions across two independent mechanisms — copper opacity to high-frequency EM (tested at GHz radio and 160 GHz CMB) and cavity-resonance frequency from the dome's own H(r) — all on the dome's own commitments.' Same content, tighter reconciliation. Not required for verification pass.

unresolved_prior_cycle.note: c4 hole #1 (cross-WIN attribution friction) — partially fixed by q357. Two of three instances (finding, tldr_verdict) now correctly cite WIN-038. The third instance (detail_verdict_text 'in WIN-029 and WIN-048, where its own H(r) at H_max produces ~8.78 Hz') was not touched. No matching open-issue currently tracks this remainder; decider should either apply the small patch directly or create an ISS for analyst follow-up.

stronger_arguments.description: If the decider opts to patch detail_verdict_text per the recommended_action above, importing one inline sentence from WIN-038's pole-floor argument would strengthen the cross-reference even further: 'H(r) is monotone decreasing in r, so c/(4·H(r)) is monotone increasing, and the pole gives the minimum quarter-wave frequency of 8.78 Hz — every off-pole point gives a higher frequency.' Lifting one line from WIN-038's lead argument tightens WIN-034's invocation of it without bloating WIN-034.

The Self-Contradicted verdict survives even if a defender attacks the 8.78 Hz number specifically: WIN-029's 22 Hz linear-average is also above 7.83 Hz, and any radial average of c/(4·H(r)) over the dome's H(r)=8537·exp(−r/8619) profile stays above the observed 7.83 Hz. The SC argument is robust to which summary statistic is preferred. WIN-034's reliance on WIN-038's specific number is rhetorical sharpness, not a load-bearing assumption.

kernel_of_truth.description: The decider's q357 patches DO land cleanly where they were applied. The WIN-029→WIN-038 cross-reference is the correct attribution (data/wins.json:WIN-038.detail_evidence: 'f(r = 0) = 299,792 / (4 × 8,537) ≈ 8.78 Hz') and the independence-claim rephrasing honestly accounts for two mechanisms (EM-opacity, cavity resonance) instantiated three times rather than overstating 'three independent' contradictions. The verification's only finding is that the patch set was scoped too narrowly to cover all three sites the c4 review flagged — not that any of the patches that did land are wrong.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: A correct partial patch does not close a flagged hole entirely; it shrinks the hole. The remaining detail_verdict_text instance still attributes 8.78 Hz to WIN-029 + WIN-048, which is the same surface mismatch the c4 review's 'a defender can pivot on this surface mismatch' caveat warned about — just at a different location. Verdict-level: the Self-Contradicted ruling is unaffected because the SC argument is robust to which Schumann statistic is preferred (see stronger_arguments[1]).

deeper_analysis: PROP-009 queue identity: queue_id=357 (target_id=WIN-034, class=verification), queue_pushed_at=2026-05-15T05:27:22Z. The two q357 patches (WIN-029→WIN-038 Schumann cross-reference, and independence-claim rephrasing) self-applied by the decider on 2026-05-15 (visible in workspace-sync commit 6259f46) land cleanly in the two fields they targeted (finding, tldr_verdict). The arithmetic (8.78 Hz at H_max; copper skin depth 2 μm at 1 GHz, 0.16 μm at 160 GHz) is independently re-verified and the verdict Self-Contradicted is unchanged. The unpatched third instance of c4 hole #1 — detail_verdict_text — is the only finding for c5, and it is severity=minor: the surface mismatch is real but does not affect verdict-level correctness, and the SC argument survives even if a defender successfully attacks the 8.78 Hz number specifically. Recommend POPPING queue 357 because the patches the decider intended to apply DID apply correctly; the residual c4 hole #1 remnant can be picked up as a separate small ISS or as part of the next analyst rewrite touching WIN-034.

citations_verified: Copper skin depth at 1 GHz ≈ 2.06 μm (computed from σ_Cu = 5.96×10^7 S/m, μ = μ_0). Matches WIN-034.detail_evidence 'skin depth ~2 μm at 1 GHz'.
Copper skin depth at 160 GHz ≈ 0.16 μm. Matches WIN-034.detail_evidence 'skin depth ~0.2 μm'.
Job 37:18 — pre-existing biblical citation, unchanged in patch.
Deuteronomy 28:23 — pre-existing biblical citation, unchanged in patch.

summary_for_decider: Queue 357 (WIN-034 q357 patch verification): Both decider patches landed cleanly where applied — WIN-029→WIN-038 Schumann cross-reference in finding and tldr_verdict; independence rephrasing 'Each test is independent — one EM-opacity argument at two frequencies, one Schumann-cavity argument'. Arithmetic re-verified, verdict Self-Contradicted holds, tests still pass (3616/0). ONE carry-over from c4 hole #1: the third instance — detail_verdict_text still says 'in WIN-029 and WIN-048, where its own H(r) at H_max produces ~8.78 Hz' — was not patched. Severity minor (surface friction, not verdict-affecting). Recommend POPPING queue 357; carry-over goes to a fresh ISS or to whoever next touches WIN-034. Singleton review (not batched). ISS-1921 collision (c4 hole #3) confirmed resolved by rename to ISS-2015.
